disclaimer: General information only — NOT legal or tax advice. Reminders only; this never files or pays. Deadlines and fees change and vary; verify with the official agency. No founder PII.

obligations.title: Texas franchise tax + Public Information Report (PIR)

fee.note: Many entities owe no tax below the no-tax-due threshold, but the report is still required. Verify current rules.

trigger.missedConsequence: Failure to file/pay can lead to forfeiture of the right to transact business and loss of good standing.
trigger.reinstatementPath: Generally: file delinquent reports and pay any tax/penalties with the Comptroller, then reinstate with the Secretary of State. Verify the current process.

cadence.dueRule: 15th day of the 5th month after the end of the accounting period (verify)

fee.note: No filing fee for the information return itself.

trigger.missedConsequence: Failure to file for three consecutive years results in automatic revocation of tax-exempt status.
trigger.reinstatementPath: Generally requires reapplying for exemption and may involve back filings. Verify the current IRS process.

cadence.dueRule: Ongoing — update promptly if the agent or address changes

fee.note: A change-of-agent filing may carry a fee. Verify.

trigger.missedConsequence: Lacking a valid registered agent can jeopardize good standing.
trigger.reinstatementPath: File the appropriate change/maintenance form with the SOS. Not advice.
